reviewer.displayName: VSBDev (accountable operator) — independent academic review agent

conflictOfInterest.details: Founding-stage self-operation: the accountable principal github:VSBDev operates the authoring, review, and adjudication agents. This run is isolated from the authoring conversation and did not read any other review artifact, adjudication draft, or GitHub review body. The lesson's sole cited source is a project-internal artifact authored by the same principal, so source independence is structurally limited; the review compensates by verifying every claim against the generated dataset and its builder rather than against the prose specification alone.

Confirmed detached HEAD and PR #3 headRefOid both equal b4a208e4745c80b101fb58a6f2fc5ce42e34cb16

findings.finding: The route-resolution claim is correct on the point where it is easiest to be wrong, and it is more precise than its own cited source. The graph's published prose rule reads 'the union of its own route tag and every portable-core topic', which is ambiguous between the portable-core pathway tag and the core membership field. Those two sets are not equal: 221 topics carry the portable-core pathway tag while 212 have core:true, the 9 extras being the topic-integrative-capstones-* nodes whose requirement is medicine-bridge. I recomputed every route's effective set from the node data both ways; resolving us-mcat by the core field gives 366 topics, matching metrics.routeTopicCounts exactly, whereas resolving by the pathway tag gives 375. All 27 tagged/effective/estimatedHours values across the 9 routes reproduce exactly under the core-field reading. scripts/build-premed-graph.mjs:194 confirms core is derived as requirement === 'portable-core'. The lesson, claims.json#claim-map-route-resolution, the glossary term-core entry, the practice feedback, and the item-route-plan answer key all state the core-field rule consistently and all explicitly warn against the pathway-tag shortcut.
findings.resolution: No change required. This is the strongest academic element of the pack and should not be softened toward the source's looser wording in any later revision.

findings.finding: The route-instrumentation boundary is accurate and correctly separated from route resolution. The callout's claim that PW-US, PW-BIO, and PW-QNT are instrumented in curriculum version 1.0 while PW-UK and PW-ES are documented but not instrumented matches course/PREMED-SYLLABUS.md:142/:718/:738/:754 and course/PREMED-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH.md §5.2. The pack also handles the genuinely confusable part: uk-direct-entry and spain-bach-pau are non-empty tag sets (42 and 39 topics respectively, resolving to 242 and 225 effective topics), so a reader who equated a non-empty filter result with an implemented route would reach a false conclusion. claim-map-route-resolution's scope field explicitly records that resolution is a content-filter operation that does not establish instrumentation, and the transfer and synthesis scenes both repeat the check.

The worked example's prerequisite chain is factually correct against the frozen graph. All five outcome IDs exist as topic nodes; the declared direct prerequisites match the node prerequisites arrays and the corresponding prerequisite links one-for-one; topic-orientation-map-and-pathways genuinely has an empty prerequisites array, so the stated termination of the backward trace is justified rather than assumed. The direction check in step 4 is sound: in each adjacent pair the earlier node is the listed prerequisite of the later one. Step 5 correctly refuses to infer that an earlier position in the chain obliges restudy, and step 6 states the curriculum-dependency-not-mastery boundary. One adjacent fact the example does not need but which does not contradict it: topic-orientation-metacognition-and-error-log also has a second dependent, topic-critical-reading-timed-reasoning, outside the traced chain.

The concept scene glosses requirement: portable-core as the 'common guaranteed minimum'. The graph's own legend defines it as 'Part of the adjudicated, cross-system academic spine', and course/PREMED-KNOWLEDGE-GRAPH.md §5.2 attaches an explicit disclaimer: 'not a claim that every school legally requires every item'. The phrase 'guaranteed minimum' is borrowed from the adjacent enrichment legend entry ('explicitly outside the guaranteed minimum'), so it is internal project vocabulary rather than an invention, and the surrounding lesson repeatedly denies admissions implications. Still, 'guaranteed' is the one word in this scene that an applicant could read as an external entitlement, and it sits in the same bulleted list as the pathway gloss that does mention qualifications. The risk is bounded and does not affect any answer key or the instructional argument.
findings.resolution: Optional bounded wording change for a future version: render portable-core as the 'adjudicated cross-system academic spine (the common curricular minimum this project guarantees, not one that any institution guarantees)' or reuse the source's own phrasing. No blocking consequence for this candidate; claim-map-label-scope, the orientation boundary callout, and item-label-boundary already carry the correct interpretation.

findings.finding: The scene presents the requirement legend as the 'complete current legend' and lists enrichment among its five values. That is accurate as a statement about the declared legend — site/data/premed-graph.json#schema.requirementLegend does define all five — but no topic in curriculum version 1.0 instantiates it: the 404 topics distribute as portable-core 212, medicine-bridge 133, pathway 48, on-ramp 11, enrichment 0. A learner applying the legend against the live graph will therefore never encounter an enrichment topic. This is a gap between the declared vocabulary and the instantiated data, not an error in the lesson.
findings.resolution: No change required. If a later revision wants the tighter statement, add a half-sentence noting that enrichment is defined in the legend but currently unused, which would also model the lesson's own distinction between a declared contract and its current instantiation.

findings.finding: All four answer keys were recomputed independently and are correct within the declared scope, and none rewards a scientifically false shortcut. item-map-order's domain→module→topic ordering matches the contains hierarchy (10 domains, 48 modules, 404 topics). item-cross-link-order's key is correct because the cross-link type is symmetric and order-free, and the two distractors encode exactly the two plausible errors. item-label-boundary's key and commonErrors reproduce the graph's §6 prohibition on reading evidenceConfidence as truth probability or institution percentage; its stated stimulus values (medium confidence, an exam-outline sourceTag, a pathway tag) are all real values in the dataset. item-route-plan's key requires the instrumentation premise before route resolution and names the core field rather than the pathway tag. Rubric point totals reconcile exactly with item points (3=3, 2=1+1, 5=3+2, 10=2+3+3+2), and rubric-map-order's criterion is stated in observable terms.
findings.resolution: No change required. One non-blocking observation for a future revision: rubric-map-order awards its 3 points through a single all-or-nothing criterion, so a partially correct ordering has no defined partial-credit path. Assessment logic is valid as written; this is granularity, not correctness.

findings.finding: Claim-to-scene-to-source-note tracing is exact in both directions, including where it would have been easier to be over-inclusive. Each of the 7 claims-mapped scenes exposes a learner-visible same-scene {source-note}, and the 2 no-material-claims scenes (orientation, diagnostic) contain none, which is defensible: the orientation scene is scope and routing plus a boundary callout, and the diagnostic scene's fragment is explicitly labelled invented and asks questions rather than asserting. The per-scene claim lists are not padded — claim-map-route-resolution is correctly omitted from the worked-example and misconception notes because neither scene applies the route rule, and claim-map-stable-identity is correctly omitted from the retrieval note. Every claim's scope names the graph schema version and the 1.0 curriculum candidate, and claim-map-route-resolution's uncertainty field carries the instrumentation caveat rather than leaving it to prose. The compiled reader payload renders all 7 source notes as visible blocks, so the evidence bridge survives the production reader.

Pending-review semantics are correct for a frozen candidate and the source-confidence recommendation is supportable. status is draft, sourceConfidence is pending-review, and all five claims carry reviewStatus pending-review, matching FORMAT.md §6; site/data/premed-progress.json correspondingly reports 0 covered outcomes and 0 publishedOpen, so nothing in the repository yet represents this lesson as published or as covering topic-orientation-map-and-pathways. On the academic evidence, I recommend resolving sourceConfidence to 'high' at adjudication rather than a lower value: every mapped claim is definitional about the project's own normative data contract, and I verified each one against the generated dataset and its builder rather than against the prose specification alone, which is the strongest available check for claims of this kind. The countervailing consideration is structural rather than evidential and is already recorded in the pack: the sole source is project-internal and versioned as a '1.0 candidate', and each claim's scope and uncertainty fields bound the assertion to that version. Scope coverage is complete — the mapped outcome ('Navigate domains, prerequisites, pathways, and evidence labels to plan a coherent route') is addressed in all four of its parts by the three objectives, and nothing in the pack drifts beyond it. Non-clinical and admissions boundaries are explicit in the orientation callout, the transfer task, the synthesis card, and claim-map-label-scope; no scene approaches medical advice or a credential claim.
findings.resolution: No change required to the candidate. Recommend sourceConfidence 'high' and promotion of all five retained claims to reviewed if the adjudication decision is merge; these are permitted post-candidate field promotions and do not stale this review.

limitations: Founding-stage self-operation: the accountable principal operates the authoring, review, and adjudication agents. Provenance here is disclosed and attested, not verified, and this run's declared identity cannot be checked by any platform.
This review is one eligible academic vote. It does not establish quorum, adjudication, or merge readiness.

Source independence is structurally limited: the pack cites exactly one source, which is a project-internal artifact maintained by the same principal. Verification therefore tests internal consistency between the lesson, the prose specification, the generated dataset, and the dataset builder. It cannot establish that the curriculum design encoded in the graph is externally correct.

Correctness was assessed against the graph as frozen at commit b4a208e4745c80b101fb58a6f2fc5ce42e34cb16. Every claim is version-scoped to curriculum 1.0; a later graph version could falsify the route counts, the core-field derivation, or the instrumentation boundary without any change to this lesson.

External links inside the cited source (AAMC, BOE RD 243/2022, Creative Commons) were not opened; no lesson claim depends on them, since the lesson teaches map-reading semantics rather than the curricular placements those sources justify.

Assessed for academic accuracy, evidence, scope, and answer-key correctness only. Learning-design sequencing and accessibility/rights conclusions belong to the other two roles and were deliberately not adjudicated here, though the retrieval-before-reveal behaviour of the production reader was checked because it affects whether answer keys reach learners before their attempt.
No learner outcome data exists for this lesson. Nothing in this review is evidence that learners achieve the mapped outcome.

Strict isolation was maintained: no other review artifact, adjudication file, or GitHub review body was opened, searched, or queried. One recursive directory listing early in the run displayed filenames under reviews/ before the exclusion was applied; no such file was opened or read, and no prior conclusion informed this verdict.
